prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "侧向摆腿" (lateral leg swings) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of an outward lateral leg swing: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground; left leg swung outward to the left side at about 45 degrees from the vertical, knee almost fully extended, foot relaxed; both hands placed on a wall in front of the body at shoulder height for balance, arms straight; torso upright, hips facing forward; controlled dynamic mobility movement; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus medius and adductor muscles (eccentric stretch). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and gluteus maximus of the supporting hip. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius; "内收肌群" pointing to the adductor muscles (under stretch); "核心 / 臀大肌" pointing to the core and supporting glute area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
